original_query: SELECT "t0"."JOB", "t1"."NAME" FROM (SELECT "EMPNO", "ENAME", "JOB", "MGR", "HIREDATE", "SAL", "COMM", "DEPTNO", "SLACKER", "DEPTNO" + "EMPNO" AS "f9" FROM "EMP" WHERE "ENAME" = 'A') AS "t0" LEFT JOIN (SELECT "DEPTNO", "NAME", "DEPTNO" + 5 AS "f2" FROM "DEPT") AS "t1" ON "t0"."JOB" = "t1"."NAME" AND "t0"."f9" = "t1"."f2" GROUP BY "t0"."JOB", "t1"."NAME"
rewritten_query:
SELECT "t0"."JOB", "t1"."NAME"
FROM (
    SELECT "JOB", "DEPTNO" + "EMPNO" AS "f9"
    FROM "EMP"
    WHERE "ENAME" = 'A'
) "t0"
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT "NAME", "DEPTNO" + 5 AS "f2"
    FROM "DEPT"
) "t1"
ON "t0"."JOB" = "t1"."NAME" AND "t0"."f9" = "t1"."f2"

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Removed unnecessary columns from subqueries to minimize data processing. The original query selected all columns from EMP but only JOB and f9 were needed.

Removed redundant GROUP BY clause. Since the join conditions ensure uniqueness (f9 is unique in t0 due to EMPNO uniqueness, and f2 is unique in t1 due to DEPTNO uniqueness), the GROUP BY operation was unnecessary.
